title: Mixed reactions in Lebanon and Israel over Iran-US ceasefire
description: The United States and Iran reached an initial agreement Monday that would extend their shaky ceasefire and lead to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, but significant challenges remain to ending the war, including whether Israel will continue its offensive in Lebanon. The deal was met by sceptism by some in Beirut who doubt if it will really bring an end to the destruction, while in Israel some would like to see the war continue.

title: G7 summit: 'Pressure will be on leaders to show support for Ukraine'

France 24's Philip Turle has the latest.

title: Israel 'has had its hands forced' by US-Iran deal
description: The US and Iran said they have agreed terms to end their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Though the text has not yet been published, its apparent call for hostilities to cease more broadly could be tricky for Israel, which was not part of the negotiations and has been waging war in Lebanon against the Iran-aligned Hezbollah group. FRANCE 24’s Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

title: Confusion in Lebanon over US-Iran deal

They also reafirmed that preventing Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons must remain a priority.

title: US-Iran deal 'what Tehran wanted'
description: US and Iran agreed to an “immediate and permanent” end to military operations under a surprise memorandum of understanding announced via mediator Pakistan on Sunday. title: World Cup 2026: Sweden make statement with commanding victory over Tunisia

Gathered in the Alps through Wednesday, they will also discuss the war in Ukraine and global economic imbalances.

Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

CAIRO: Egyptian President Abdel Fattah El-Sisi said Thursday his country was in an economic “state of near-emergency” as a result of the Middle East war, warning of runaway inflation.
	The Arab world’s most populous nation has not been physically impacted by the US and Israeli war with Iran, which has seen strikes on Egypt’s wealthy Gulf allies and paralyzed trade through the vital Strait of Hormuz.

BEIRUT: Lebanon faces what officials described as “one of the most dangerous moments in recent memory” after Hezbollah launched attacks on Israeli forces from areas both south and north of the Litani River, triggering a renewed Israeli air and ground campaign.

title: Israel decided to kill Khamenei in November, defense minister says
description:
JERUSALEM: Israel took the decision to kill Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in November and was planning to carry out the operation around six months later, Defense Minister Israel Katz said on Thursday.
	Khamenei was killed in the first hours of the US-Israeli air campaign that began on Saturday in the first assassination of a country’s top ruler by an airstrike.

The post Israel braces for Iranian missile fire after strike on Hezbollah target in Beirut appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Italian Astronaut Luca Parmitano Becomes First European Assigned To NASA’s Artemis Mission
description: Italian astronaut Luca Parmitano will serve as pilot on Artemis III, marking a milestone for Europe’s participation in the program which will bring once again a human on the Moon. NASA has announced the four-member crew assigned to the Artemis III mission, selecting Italian astronaut Luca Parmitano as the missions pilot. The selection makes Parmitano []

title: Trump to Axios: Netanyahu has "no fucking judgment" but Iran deal still on
description: President Trump tells Axios the U.S.–Iran deal to end the war is still on track for Sunday despite Israel's strike in Beirut and the Iranian threat to retaliate. Why it matters: The president is racing to save a deal that nearly collapsed the moment Israel struck Beirut, leaning on private diplomacy and public messaging to get it signed. Iranian officials haven't confirmed a deal is expected to be signed today."It shook it up. It delayed the sign

title: Trump faults Israel's Beirut strike, urges stand-down as Iran threatens U.S. deal
description: A drone strike on northern Israel set off a chain reaction Sunday: Israel hit Hezbollah targets in Beirut in retaliation, and Iran is now threatening to strike back.Why it matters: The escalation lands hours before the the U.S. and Iran are supposed to sign a deal to end their war, and now Iranian officials have signaled Israel's attack could scuttle the agreement. title: UK intercepts Russian shadow fleet vessel in Channel: ministry
description: British forces on Sunday intercepted a sanctioned oil tanker belonging to Russia's shadow fleet in the English Channel, the defence ministry said, in a six-hour operation hailed by Kyiv.

title: Trump vows Mideast deal is on, calls for no more attacks
description: US President Donald Trump on Sunday said Israeli strikes on Beirut "should not have happened" as he vowed a regional peace deal was at hand, though he did not confirm his earlier claim it would be signed during the day. France 24's Noga Tarnopolsky tells us more.

title: ‘Unacceptable’: London protesters slam illegal Israeli settlement land sale
description: Demonstrators denounce event promoting Israeli settlement land and property sales as a violation of international law.

title: UK forces seize suspected Russian oil tanker accused of funding Ukraine war
description: British armed forces intercepted an oil tanker believed to be part of Russia’s sanctioned shadow fleet.

title: Trump condemns Israel’s attack on Beirut, says Iran deal still close
description: Donald Trump says there should be no more attacks by Israel or Hezbollah on special day when deal could be signed.

title: With a Deal Seemingly Close, the U.S. Faces an Iran More Willing to Withstand Pressure
description: Iran’s new, more militaristic leaders have already survived the worst that America and Israel can deliver, and seem readier to take risks.

title: Russia Is Rich in Ballistic Missiles. Ukraine Is Short of Ways to Stop Them.
description: Ukraine is running out of American-made Patriot air-defense interceptors, and is pleading for more.
